description: The <tt>root</tt> user should have a primary group of 0.
rationale: To help ensure that root-owned files are not inadvertently exposed to other users.
ocil_clause: root has a primary gid not equal to zero or other non-system accounts has a primary gid equal to zero
ocil:
To verify that root's primary group is zero run the following command: 
    awk -F: '($1 !~ /^(sync|shutdown|halt|operator)/ && $4=="0") {print $1":"$4}' /etc/passwd
The command should return: 
    root:0
title: Verify Root Has A Primary GID 0
